_meta.name: ALM 14B r8a stacked launch — MFU ceiling measurement
_meta.authorization: user go — 2 H100 max, 여유시간 페이스, HONEST reporting
_meta.status: MEASURED — smoke PASS, 150-step steady state captured, target 75% NOT reached, pod fleet variance dominant

objective: Stack all compatible speedup flags on top of best batch/seq config and measure achieved MFU on ALM 14B LoRA to confirm practical bf16 ceiling 75-85%

pod_selection.selection_rationale: ONLY pod available — fast pod ubzp169igb7tns (r7 pod) was terminated after r7 completed 4000 steps. xhq9b2c8fljdyo is 31.5% slower per pod fleet variance measurement (see r8_ceiling_confirm). This caveat dominates the measurement.

pod_fleet_variance_caveat.finding: Same software stack measured 33.3 step/min on this pod vs 48.6 on ubzp169igb7tns at step 50 (-31%)
pod_fleet_variance_caveat.implication: MFU measured on this pod is a LOWER BOUND relative to r7's ceiling. Pod-variance-adjusted values are annotated below.

gated_flags.ALM_COMPILE_UNBLOCK=1: set but NO-OP under --unsloth per trainer gate (line 363: if ALM_COMPILE_UNBLOCK and not args.unsloth)

batch_seq_config.source: batch_seq_sweep_2026_04_15 OPTIMAL (b=8 s=1024 g=1 = 70.9% MFU predicted on r6 pod)

unsloth_fast_lora_kernel_status.r7_baseline_status: DISABLED (0 QKV / 0 O / 0 MLP layers patched with fast kernels due to lora_dropout=0.05)
unsloth_fast_lora_kernel_status.r8a_status: PARTIALLY ENABLED (48 QKV + 48 O + 0 MLP patched with fast kernels due to lora_dropout=0.0)
unsloth_fast_lora_kernel_status.mlp_still_disabled_reason: Unsloth cannot patch MLP layers with manual autograd because Qwen2 MLP uses bias term (Unsloth warning on launch)
unsloth_fast_lora_kernel_status.log_excerpt: Unsloth 2026.4.4 patched 48 layers with 48 QKV layers, 48 O layers and 0 MLP layers.

install_status.unsloth: 2026.4.4 (from prior r7+r8 install, no-op this run)
install_status.fused_ce_disabled_reason: Unsloth owns the CE path when --unsloth is active; liger fused CE would clash. Per training/train_alm_14b.hexa audit (commit 7db69044).
install_status.transformer_engine: NOT INSTALLED — blocked by wheel 404 + source build cudnn.h missing (per r8_partial blockers)
install_status.flash_attention_2: Xformers fallback — FA2 wheel broken on torch 2.10+cu128

build_method.option_used: Option B (direct runner patch on pod)
build_method.option_a_failure_reason: clang 'bracket nesting level exceeded maximum of 256' on /tmp/train_alm_14b_bin.c:22 — transpile succeeds but clang cannot compile. Known issue per project_session_20260412_night post #23 (bracket-depth fix was partial).
build_method.option_a_fallback_attempted: hexa run emitted /tmp/_train_alm_14b_runner.py locally (567 lines) but uses fused_linear_cross_entropy=True UNCONDITIONALLY which conflicts with Unsloth's CE path — would have been a regression.
build_method.option_b_basis: Pod already had /workspace/train_alm_14b_r8.py (574 lines) with the liger+unsloth compat patch applied during r8_partial session. Copied to /workspace/train_alm_14b_r8a.py (md5 c3c7038cf091b957f9d484b170c9e772, no further modification). Fresh run via /workspace/launch_r8a_smoke.sh + /workspace/launch_r8a_150.sh.
build_method.compat_patch_present: line 343: fused_linear_cross_entropy=(not _unsloth_on) — correctly yields False when --unsloth is active

smoke_metrics.verdict: PASS — loss descending, no NaN, no OOM, GPU 100% util, VRAM 43 GB matches sweep prediction (42.1 GB for b8s1024g1). Same training rate as --unsloth-only sweep (37.7 step/min) at step 20, so pre-steady-state the stack deltas are inside warmup noise.

authoritative_measurement.source: interval s20-s150 (post-warmup steady state, 130 steps of real training)

alt_measurement_step100_cum.rationale: Apples-to-apples with r7 which measured step_100 cumulative 51.1 step/min on fast pod

150step_steady_state_metrics.convergence_verdict: HEALTHY — loss 15.62 → 15.02 (-0.6), monotone decreasing past step 90, no NaN/Inf, warmup cosine schedule tracking normally

vs_r8_partial_unsloth_only_same_pod.reference: r8_partial b8s1024g1 --unsloth only on xhq9b2c8fljdyo (37.7 step/min, 46.5% MFU, sweep measurement)
vs_r8_partial_unsloth_only_same_pod.verdict: REAL but MODEST. The liger + lora_dropout=0 + 96-layer fast-LoRA-kernel unlock delivered +10% relative throughput on top of --unsloth only, which maps to +1.6 MFU points on this slow pod. On the fast pod the uplift would scale similarly to ~+1.6 MFU points above r7's 62.6%, placing the stacked ceiling at ~64.2%.

vs_r7_raw_direct.reference: r7 step_100 cumulative on fast pod ubzp169igb7tns (51.1 step/min, 62.6% MFU)
vs_r7_raw_direct.verdict: APPEARS REGRESSED but this is CROSS-POD comparison — invalidated by 31.5% pod fleet variance.

vs_r7_pod_variance_adjusted.reference: r7 projected onto slow pod: 51.1 * 0.685 = 35.0 step/min
vs_r7_pod_variance_adjusted.verdict: +18.6% uplift relative to r7 pod-adjusted. Decomposes as: (a) ~+10% from b8s1024g1 config vs r7's suboptimal b1g8 (sweep finding); (b) remaining ~+7% from liger + lora_dropout=0 + QKV/O fast-kernel unlock.

vs_sweep_optimal_same_pod.verdict: This is the CLEAN A/B (same pod, same config, only stack difference). Stacked flags = +10.1% rel = +1.6 MFU points.

ceiling_verdict.honest_assessment: Target 75% MFU NOT reached on either pod. On the slow pod xhq9b2c8fljdyo we measured 48.9% steady-state. Cross-pod adjustment against r7 baseline suggests the stacked ceiling on the fast pod would be ~64%, still 10+ points below target. The liger + lora_dropout=0 stack delivered the expected +10% uplift over --unsloth alone, but there is no new lever available to close the remaining gap without FP8 (blocked by TE wheel) or MLP-path fast LoRA (blocked by Qwen's MLP bias term that Unsloth refuses to patch).

practical_bf16_ceiling_revised.published_75pct_realistic: NO — published 75% (NeMo Llama2-70B on SXM cluster) is not achievable on single-GPU Qwen2.5-14B LoRA without FP8 or different base architecture

blockers_for_higher_mfu.status: BLOCKED — TE 2.13.0 wheel 404 for torch 2.10+cu128, source build fails cudnn.h missing
blockers_for_higher_mfu.workaround: Pin torch==2.9 on fresh pod and install TE 2.12 pre-built wheels, OR apt install libcudnn-dev to provide headers
blockers_for_higher_mfu.risk: Torch 2.9 downgrade invalidates r7's stack, so the r7 ceiling would need re-measurement post-downgrade

blockers_for_higher_mfu.status: BLOCKED — Qwen2 MLP uses bias term (gate_proj/up_proj/down_proj) which Unsloth's manual autograd cannot patch
blockers_for_higher_mfu.workaround: NONE short-term. Alternative: custom triton MLP kernel, or LoRA on MLP projections via peft standard path (but that disables FastLanguageModel)
blockers_for_higher_mfu.upstream_note: Unsloth's warning: 'Not an error, but Unsloth cannot patch MLP layers with our manual autograd engine since either LoRA adapters are not enabled or a bias term (like in Qwen) is used.'

blockers_for_higher_mfu.status: OBSERVED — same software stack measured 33.3 step/min on slow pod vs 48.6 on fast pod (31%)
blockers_for_higher_mfu.workaround: Reserve the fast pod ubzp169igb7tns for ceiling confirmation (currently terminated, must re-provision)
blockers_for_higher_mfu.cost_implication: Ceiling measurements have ~31% margin of error if pod_id is not controlled; all session measurements MUST tag pod_id

cost_estimate.vs_budget_3to5_usd: UNDER — used only 8% of $5 upper budget

references: training/train_alm_14b.hexa (hexa launcher canonical — clang build blocked by bracket depth)
next_actions: 1. (optional) Provision fresh pod + torch 2.9 + TE install → re-run r8a with --fp8 → measure +34-40% ceiling
2. (optional) Retest r8a on a different freshly-provisioned H100 pod to characterize pod fleet variance tail (is 31% the worst case or is there more?)
3. Record the 65% fast-pod practical bf16 ceiling as the session's final answer — stop further bf16 micro-optimization, pivot to fp8 or multi-GPU DDP
4. Fix training/train_alm_14b.hexa clang bracket-depth so future sessions can use Option A (hexa build → native binary) instead of Option B (direct py patch)
